mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates, lowering present value of future equity cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative equity repricing over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >55% of triggered instances during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and real sales of autos and light trucks, where GDP expands while auto sales contract, indicates weakening consumer demand that depresses corporate earnings and yields negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p>0.10) in out-of-sample panel regression over 24 months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output and profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive returns in >60% of observations during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising inflation and rising policy rates creates stagflationary pressure that compresses real discount rates and equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL both increase MoM
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict direction with accuracy >52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which supports higher equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative or below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts below 1.3 million units signals a collapse in construction demand and related consumer spending, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >58% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and expansion in the unemployment rate confirms a broad-based economic slowdown that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines MoM and UNRATE rises MoM
failure_condition: Signal accuracy drops below 50% or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample tests.
